summary: Moved a small Ice Climbers assembly slice out of asm/melee/ft/chara/fticeclimber.s into fticeclimber1.c, with real C matches for lbl_8011F3D8 and func_8011F6FC plus MWERKS_GEKKO inline-asm carryovers for still-unmatched nearby routines. The C work clarified Nana-related fighter state at sa.nana.x222C, callback cleanup for x21E4_callback_OnDeath2 and x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage, and ftcmd var access at x2204_ftcmd_var1. The PR also added missing prototypes/includes needed by the moved code and resolved issue #601, depending on #713.
what_changed: Deleted 236 lines of raw assembly covering lbl_8011F3D8 through func_8011F6FC from asm/melee/ft/chara/fticeclimber.s.
Added C implementation of lbl_8011F3D8(HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj), using func_80082708, GET_FIGHTER, func_802C17DC, func_800CC730, and Nana-specific fighter state fields.
Added C implementation of func_8011F6FC(HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj), returning fp->x2204_ftcmd_var1 when a fighter object and Fighter exist, otherwise returning true.
Kept lbl_8011F454, lbl_8011F500, and func_8011F68C as MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm inside fticeclimber1.c rather than leaving them in the separate asm file.
Added fticeclimber1.h declarations for lbl_8011F3D8, lbl_8011F454, and func_8011F6FC.
Added item header declarations for func_802C16F8(HSD_GObj*) and func_802C1590(HSD_GObj*), while func_802C17DC(uint) was already declared.
Included melee/lb/lbunknown_001.h in fticeclimber1.c, apparently to support references from the moved code/inline asm.
smart_moves: Used GET_FIGHTER(fighter_gobj) rather than manual gobj->user_data offset access, consistent with the PR dependency on #713 and making fighter object retrieval clearer.

Matched lbl_8011F3D8 by preserving the original control flow: only run cleanup when func_80082708 returns false, call func_802C17DC on a non-null Nana object pointer, then clear Nana object and damage/death callbacks only if the object pointer is still the same.
Used a second GET_FIGHTER call after func_802C17DC in lbl_8011F3D8, matching the assembly pattern that reloads the Fighter from the GObj after the item/object cleanup call.
Staged unmatched neighboring routines as inline asm in the C file under MWERKS_GEKKO, allowing the standalone asm block to shrink while preserving exact codegen for functions not yet converted to C.
Added headers/prototypes alongside the C migration so future callers can reference the newly migrated symbols without ad hoc externs.
decomp_lessons: For fighter code, reloading GET_FIGHTER after a call can be necessary for matching when the original assembly reloads gobj->user_data instead of reusing a previous local.

When a decomp slice contains both matchable and stubborn functions, moving the whole contiguous region into the C file with inline asm placeholders can reduce asm file fragmentation without forcing all functions to be solved at once.

Callback cleanup patterns should preserve alias checks: this PR only clears x222C, x21E4_callback_OnDeath2, and x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage when the stored object pointer still matches after func_802C17DC.
Null-handling behavior is part of the match and semantics: func_8011F6FC returns true if the HSD_GObj is NULL or if GET_FIGHTER yields NULL.
Header hygiene matters in incremental decomp PRs: newly referenced item helper functions and fighter-local functions were declared as part of the same change.
naming_conventions: Kept unmatched or unnamed Ice Climber functions under address-derived labels such as lbl_8011F3D8, lbl_8011F454, lbl_8011F500, func_8011F68C, and func_8011F6FC.
Used the existing character file naming convention fticeclimber1.c and fticeclimber1.h under src/melee/ft/chara/ftIceClimber/.
Used Fighter* locals named fp1 and fp2 in lbl_8011F3D8 to distinguish pre-call and post-call fighter reloads.
Used fighter_gobj as the HSD_GObj* parameter name in the newly decompiled functions.
Referenced Nana-specific state through fp->sa.nana.x222C rather than raw offset 0x222C.
Referenced callbacks through fp->cb.x21E4_callback_OnDeath2 and fp->cb.x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted lbl_8011F3D8 from assembly to C while preserving reloads and comparisons visible in the original assembly.
Used explicit unsigned zero constants such as 0U for pointer/state clearing in lbl_8011F3D8.
Used an explicit `(u32)` cast on both sides of the x222C comparison, likely to force the same comparison/codegen shape as the original `cmplw` pointer/integer comparison.
Used MWERKS_GEKKO `asm void ...`, `#pragma push`, `#pragma pop`, `nofralloc`, and `clang-format off` to embed exact assembly for functions not yet matched in C.
Replaced small-data references inside inline asm as direct symbol loads such as `lfs f0, lbl_804D9838` and declared `extern f32 const lbl_804D9848;` for another float constant.
Preserved original branch labels inside inline asm blocks for lbl_8011F454, lbl_8011F500, and func_8011F68C.
review_feedback: No line review comments were present in the slice.
r-burns approved the PR with an empty review body on 2023-01-26.
The PR body states it depends on #713 and resolves #601.
An issue comment only pinged @r-burns; no technical feedback was included.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully decompile lbl_8011F454, lbl_8011F500, or func_8011F68C from the inline asm added here.
Search for other uses of sa.nana.x222C to confirm its meaning and whether it should receive a stronger field name.
Search for x2204_ftcmd_var1 in other fighter files to compare null fallback behavior and bool return conventions.
Review PR #713 for the GET_FIGHTER changes this PR depended on.
Search issue #601 to understand the exact matching target or progress tracking resolved by this PR.
